title: Background Remover: Paint-to-Select + Erase Button Workflow

requirements.text: In the Background Remover module (frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover.tsx and frontend/src/modules/BackgroundRemover/BrushToolPanel.tsx), update the brush/paint tool workflow so that: (1) The user activates the Paint tool button to enter paint mode and paints over the area of the image they want to remove (the selected area is highlighted with a semi-transparent red overlay as already implemented). (2) After painting the desired area, the user clicks a dedicated 'Erase' button (distinct from the existing erase-mode toggle) that processes the canvas and removes only the painted/selected pixels — making them transparent (PNG output) or replacing them with the chosen solid color (JPG output). (3) The 'Erase' button must be prominently visible in the BrushToolPanel or the main toolbar whenever there are painted brush strokes on the canvas (i.e., the mask is non-empty). (4) The existing 'Remove Background' flow in RemovalPanel.tsx must continue to work as before for auto-detect and full background removal. (5) The Erase button must be styled using the existing tool-btn and teal/cyan accent CSS classes consistent with the glassmorphism design system.

quotes: user paint button and select image area after select area click to erase button for removing background from photo

acceptanceCriteria: A Paint button is visible in the Background Remover module that activates brush/paint mode on the canvas.
When paint mode is active, the user can paint over any area of the image and the painted region is highlighted with a semi-transparent red overlay.
After painting, an 'Erase' button becomes visible/enabled in the panel.

Clicking the Erase button removes (erases) only the pixels covered by the painted brush strokes — making them transparent for PNG output or replacing them with the selected background color for JPG output.
The result is immediately reflected in the canvas preview and the before/after preview.
The Erase button is disabled or hidden when no brush strokes have been painted (empty mask).
The existing Auto Detect and Remove Background buttons in RemovalPanel continue to function independently.
The Erase button follows the existing tool-btn glassmorphism styling with teal/cyan accent colors.

constraints: Do not break the existing brush tool undo functionality or the erase-mode toggle (for unpainting brush strokes).
Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
All processing must remain client-side — no backend involvement.
Maintain the existing glassmorphism dark-first design system.
nonGoals: Adding AI or ML-based automatic background detection.
Changing the existing Auto Detect or Remove Background button behavior.
Modifying any other module (Image Editor, PDF Converter, Image Converter, Passport Photo, Video Downloader).
